info.title: Kuvasz Uptime
info.description: Kuvasz Uptime is an open-source, self-hostable uptime monitoring and status page service.

id.description: A unique identifier for an integration, formatted as 'type:name'.

name.description: Name of the integration. Must be unique in the context of type.

global.description: Whether the integration is global (applies to all monitors by default)

id.description: A unique identifier for an integration, formatted as 'type:name'.

name.description: Name of the integration. Must be unique in the context of type.

global.description: Whether the integration is global (applies to all monitors by default)

fromAddress.description: The email address from which notifications are sent

toAddress.description: The email address to which notifications are sent

period.description: The period that was used for the calculation. An ISO-8601 Duration string.

incidents.description: Total number of incidents recorded across all monitors.

affectedMonitors.description: Total number of monitors that have been affected by incidents.

uptimeRatio.description: The percentage of uptime across all monitors.

totalDowntimeSeconds.description: Total downtime in seconds across all monitors.

name.description: Unique name for the monitor, e.g., 'My Website Monitor'
name.x-size-message: Monitor name must not be blank

url.x-pattern-message: URL must be a valid HTTP(S) URI

uptimeCheckInterval.description: The interval in seconds at which the monitor checks for uptime
uptimeCheckInterval.x-minimum-message: Uptime check interval must be at least {value} seconds
uptimeCheckInterval.x-not-null-message: Uptime check interval must not be null

enabled.description: Whether the monitor is enabled. If false, the monitor will not perform checks.

sslCheckEnabled.description: Whether the monitor checks the SSL certificate for expiry and validity.

requestMethod.description: The HTTP method used for the uptime check

latencyHistoryEnabled.description: Whether latency history is enabled for the monitor

forceNoCache.description: Whether to send a force no-cache headers in the request

followRedirects.description: Whether to follow redirects during the uptime check

sslExpiryThreshold.description: The threshold in days for SSL certificate expiry checks
sslExpiryThreshold.x-minimum-message: SSL expiry threshold must be greater than or equal to 0 days
sslExpiryThreshold.x-not-null-message: SSL expiry threshold must not be null

integrations.description: List of integrations explicitly assigned to the monitor, e.g. "email:my-email-notification"

expectedStatusCodes.description: List of expected HTTP status codes for the monitor. If the response code is not in this list, the monitor will be marked as DOWN. By default, every 2xx status will be considered as UP. 1xx, 2xx, 3xx and 4xx codes are supported.

responseTimeThresholdMillis.description: The threshold in milliseconds for the response time. If the response time exceeds this value, the monitor will be marked as DOWN.
responseTimeThresholdMillis.x-maximum-message: Response time threshold must be less than or equal to {value} milliseconds
responseTimeThresholdMillis.x-minimum-message: Response time threshold must be greater than 0 milliseconds

expectedKeyword.description: A keyword that is expected to be present in the response body. If the keyword is not found, the monitor will be marked as DOWN.

expectedKeywordCaseSensitive.description: Whether the expected keyword check is case-sensitive. If false, the check will be case-insensitive.

expectedKeywordNegated.description: Whether the expected keyword check is negated. If true, the monitor will be marked as DOWN if the keyword is found in the response body.

requestHeaders.description: Custom HTTP headers to be sent with the request. It is a map of header names to values, null values are not allowed.

expectedHeaders.description: Expected HTTP headers in the response. If any of these headers are missing, the monitor will be marked as DOWN. It is a map of header names to values, null values are not allowed. The check will be case-insensitive for the header names, but the values must match exactly

requestBody.description: The body of the request to be sent, which is used for POST, PATCH and PUT requests. Currently only valid JSON bodies are supported.

failureCountThreshold.description: The threshold for consecutive failures before the monitor is marked as DOWN
failureCountThreshold.x-minimum-message: Failure count threshold must be greater than 0
failureCountThreshold.x-not-null-message: Failure count threshold must not be null

name.description: Unique name for the monitor, e.g., 'My Website Monitor'

uptimeCheckInterval.description: The interval in seconds at which the monitor checks for uptime

enabled.description: Whether the monitor is enabled. If false, the monitor will not perform checks.

sslCheckEnabled.description: Whether the monitor checks the SSL certificate for expiry and validity.

createdAt.description: The creation timestamp of the monitor

updatedAt.description: The last updated timestamp of the monitor

uptimeStatus.description: The current uptime status of the monitor. If it's null, the monitor has not been checked yet.

uptimeStatusStartedAt.description: The timestamp when the uptime status was last changed

lastUptimeCheck.description: The timestamp when the last uptime check was performed

nextUptimeCheck.description: The timestamp when the next uptime check is scheduled

sslStatus.description: The current SSL status of the monitor. If it's null, the monitor has not been checked yet.

sslStatusStartedAt.description: The timestamp when the SSL status was last changed

lastSSLCheck.description: The timestamp when the last SSL check was performed

nextSSLCheck.description: The timestamp when the next SSL check is scheduled

uptimeError.description: The error message if the last uptime check failed

sslError.description: The error message if the last SSL check failed

requestMethod.description: The HTTP method used for the uptime check

latencyHistoryEnabled.description: Whether latency history is enabled for the monitor

forceNoCache.description: Whether to send a force no-cache headers in the request

followRedirects.description: Whether to follow redirects during the uptime check

sslExpiryThreshold.description: The threshold in days for SSL certificate expiry checks

failureCountThreshold.description: The threshold for consecutive failures before the monitor is marked as DOWN

sslValidUntil.description: The timestamp until which the SSL certificate is valid

integrations.description: List of integrations explicitly assigned to the monitor, e.g. "email:my-email-notification"

items.description: A unique identifier for an integration, formatted as 'type:name'.

effectiveIntegrations.description: List of integrations that are effective for the monitor, including global integrations

createdAt.description: The creation timestamp of the monitor

updatedAt.description: The last updated timestamp of the monitor

latencyHistoryEnabled.description: Whether recording of latency is enabled for this monitor

latencyStats.description: Latency related statistics of the monitor in the given period

uptimeHistory.description: Uptime related statistics of the monitor in the given period

latencyLogs.description: All the latency logs recorded for the monitor in the given period

requestBody.description: The body of the request to be sent, which is used for POST, PATCH and PUT requests. Currently only valid JSON bodies are supported.

failureCountThreshold.description: The threshold for consecutive failures before the monitor is marked as DOWN
failureCountThreshold.x-minimum-message: Failure count threshold must be greater than 0

actual.description: The actual monitoring statistics for the current state of the monitors.

history.description: The historical monitoring statistics, summarizing incidents and uptime ratios over time.

uptimeStats.description: Statistics about the current state of uptime for all monitors.

sslStats.description: Statistics about the current state of SSL certificates for all monitors.

total.description: Total number of monitors currently being monitored.

inProgress.description: Number of monitors currently in progress (e.g., waiting for first check).

lastIncident.description: The timestamp of the last incident detected across all monitors.

invalid.description: Number of SSL certificates that are currently invalid or expired.

valid.description: Number of SSL certificates that are valid and not close to expiry.

willExpire.description: Number of SSL certificates that are close to expiry (within the threshold).

inProgress.description: Number of SSL certificates that has not been checked yet.

uptimeStats.description: Statistics about the historical uptime of all monitors.

id.description: Unique identifier for the uptime event

status.description: The status of the uptime event

error.description: The error that occurred during the uptime check, if any

startedAt.description: The timestamp when the uptime event started

endedAt.description: The timestamp when the uptime event ended, if applicable

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the uptime event was updated

monitorId.description: ID of the monitor this incident belongs to

monitorName.description: Name of the monitor this incident belongs to

isMonitorEnabled.description: Whether the monitor related to the incident is enabled

details.description: Details about the incident, e.g. error message

endedAt.description: When the incident ended, null if ongoing

updatedAt.description: When the incident was last updated

id.description: A unique identifier for an integration, formatted as 'type:name'.
Unique identifier for the integration that can be used as a reference on a monitor

type.description: Type of the integration, e.g., EMAIL, WEBHOOK, etc.

name.description: Name of the integration, e.g., 'slack-team-devops', 'email-ops-global', etc.

enabled.description: Whether the integration is enabled or not

global.description: Whether the integration is global or not

id.description: Unique identifier of the latency log

latencyInMs.description: The latency in milliseconds recorded for the monitor

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the latency was recorded

averageLatencyInMs.description: The average latency in milliseconds for the monitor

minLatencyInMs.description: The minimum latency in milliseconds for the monitor

maxLatencyInMs.description: The maximum latency in milliseconds for the monitor

p90LatencyInMs.description: The 90th percentile latency in milliseconds for the monitor

p95LatencyInMs.description: The 95th percentile latency in milliseconds for the monitor

p99LatencyInMs.description: The 99th percentile latency in milliseconds for the monitor

id.description: A unique identifier for an integration, formatted as 'type:name'.

name.description: Name of the integration. Must be unique in the context of type.

global.description: Whether the integration is global (applies to all monitors by default)

name.description: Unique name for the monitor, e.g., 'My Website Monitor'
name.x-size-message: Monitor name must not be blank

heartbeatInterval.description: The interval in seconds at which the monitor expects to receive heartbeats
heartbeatInterval.x-minimum-message: Heartbeat interval must be at least {value} seconds
heartbeatInterval.x-not-null-message: Heartbeat interval must not be null

gracePeriod.description: The grace period in seconds after the heartbeat interval during which a missed heartbeat will not mark the monitor as DOWN
gracePeriod.x-minimum-message: Grace period must be greater than or equal to 0 seconds
gracePeriod.x-not-null-message: Grace period must not be null

enabled.description: Whether the monitor is enabled. If false, the monitor will not perform checks.

integrations.description: List of integrations explicitly assigned to the monitor, e.g. "email:my-email-notification"

clientSecret.description: The unique client secret used to identify & authenticate heartbeats
clientSecret.x-size-message: Client secret must be at least {min} characters long
clientSecret.x-not-null-message: Client secret must not be null

failureCountThreshold.description: The threshold for consecutive failures before the monitor is marked as DOWN
failureCountThreshold.x-minimum-message: Failure count threshold must be greater than 0
failureCountThreshold.x-not-null-message: Failure count threshold must not be null

lastHeartbeat.description: The timestamp of the last successful heartbeat

nextExpectedHeartbeat.description: The timestamp until which the next heartbeat is expected

uptimeError.description: The error message if the last uptime check failed

integrations.description: List of integrations explicitly assigned to the monitor, e.g. "email:my-email-notification"

status.description: The status of the uptime event

error.description: The error that occurred during the uptime check, if any

id.description: Unique identifier for the SSL event

status.description: The status of the SSL certificate

error.description: The error that occurred during the SSL check, if any

startedAt.description: The timestamp when the SSL event started

sslValidUntil.description: The timestamp the SSL certificate is valid until

endedAt.description: The timestamp when the SSL event ended, if applicable

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the SSL event was updated

eventDataRetentionDays.description: Number of days to retain event data

latencyDataRetentionDays.description: Number of days to retain latency data

editabilityState.description: Whether the application is in read-only mode (i.e. monitors are configured via YAML

updateChecksEnabled.description: Whether automatic update checks are enabled

httpCheckTimeoutSeconds.description: The HTTP uptime checks' read timeout

areHttpMonitorsReadOnly.description: Whether the HTTP monitors are in read-only mode

areStatusPagesReadOnly.description: Whether the status pages are in read-only mode

arePushMonitorsReadOnly.description: Whether the push monitors are in read-only mode

accessTokenMaxAge.description: The maximum age of the access token in seconds

exportEnabled.description: Whether the metrics exporting is generally enabled

url.description: The endpoint where the metrics will be published

step.description: The step for the metrics reporting as an ISO 8601 duration string

descriptions.description: Whether descriptions are included in the export

sslExpiry.description: Whether SSL certificate expiry exporter is enabled

httpLatestLatency.description: Whether HTTP latest latency exporter is enabled

httpUptimeStatus.description: Whether HTTP monitor status exporter is enabled

sslStatus.description: Whether SSL status exporter is enabled

id.description: A unique identifier for an integration, formatted as 'type:name'.

name.description: Name of the integration. Must be unique in the context of type.

global.description: Whether the integration is global (applies to all monitors by default)

title.x-size-message: Status page title must not be blank

slug.description: Unique slug of the status page, used in the URL
slug.x-pattern-message: Status page slug must be 1-50 characters long and can only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores
slug.x-size-message: Status page slug must not be blank

customLogoUrl.description: The absolute URL of the custom logo displayed on the status page

customFaviconUrl.description: The absolute URL of the custom favicon displayed on the status page. Only PNG format is supported.

public.description: Whether the status page is publicly accessible

monitors.description: Set of monitor IDs that are included in the status page

slug.description: Unique slug of the status page, used in the URL

customLogoUrl.description: The absolute URL of the custom logo displayed on the status page

customFaviconUrl.description: The absolute URL of the custom favicon displayed on the status page. Only PNG format is supported.

public.description: Whether the status page is publicly accessible

monitors.description: Set of monitor IDs that are included in the status page

items.description: A unique identifier for a monitor, formatted as 'type:name'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the status page was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of when the status page was last updated

title.x-size-message: Status page title must not be blank

slug.description: Unique slug of the status page, used in the URL
slug.x-pattern-message: Status page slug must be 1-50 characters long and can only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores
slug.x-size-message: Status page slug must not be blank

customLogoUrl.description: The absolute URL of the custom logo displayed on the status page

customFaviconUrl.description: The absolute URL of the custom favicon displayed on the status page. Only PNG format is supported.

public.description: Whether the status page is publicly accessible

monitors.description: Set of monitor IDs that are included in the status page

items.description: A unique identifier for a monitor, formatted as 'type:name'.

id.description: A unique identifier for an integration, formatted as 'type:name'.

name.description: Name of the integration. Must be unique in the context of type.

global.description: Whether the integration is global (applies to all monitors by default)

chatId.description: The chat ID for Telegram notifications

installedVersion.description: The currently installed version of Kuvasz

latestVersion.description: The latest available version of Kuvasz, if update checks are enabled

latestVersionDetails.description: A link to the changelog or release notes for the latest version, if update checks are enabled

isUpToDate.description: Whether the installed version is up to date. If update checks are disabled, it's always true
